item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Woo, upvote 200! Love me a good temporal department scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You bet your ass this just got into Xyank canon.

Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article gives me a picture. MTF was fighting with SCP-3797-ARC in despair, seems like no one can prevent SCP-3797-ARC, and the end of the world is coming.

Then instances of the Temporal Anomalies Department insignia appeared and terminated SCP-3797-ARC.

It's hope and hero.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh 3 minutes haha.

Well, of course it'll be generic. But maybe it could be a bit different if anyone takes up on it. That's why it's such a smart idea to leave it unincluded for now. (and maybe it'll stay that way.)

Among what we already have, it coulda been, what, 239? Scarlet King? That's why, imo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh woops the year on the second date was in error, it's meant to die exactly three minutes after awakening. Date should be 2015, not 2016. >_<

I had written some more details about SCP-3797-ARC, but it mostly boiled down to an uniteresting, very powerful sleeping demigod. Not including it in this document was an intentional choice, because I didn't think what I had in mind would make for an interesting story - what's interesting is how it was killed, not what it was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just sweet.

I wonder if anything interesting can be done with SCP-3797-ARC. It stayed around for a year, fighting immortals and reality benders, then it died, EXACTLY 1 year and 3 minutes after it appeared.

The minimalism of the Once and Future Gun means that details on SCP-3797-ARC will have to be sparse, too. Fleshing out 3797-ARC too much means fleshing out 3797 itself. (Which could be good or bad.) For now, though, 3797-ARC is generic enough to make me think there could be some potential?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just like the weapon used by my PL-120, but I still like the article enough to upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same issues as when I last looked at it - the idea is clever, but in order for it to actually work well as an article, you need a narrative that the reader can follow rather than just relying on the time-travel gimmick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The non-link is supposed to look enough like a real link to convey the idea of "this is meant to be a link" but not enough that people actually mistake it for a link.

(For the record, I've used bb0011 before, on SCP-3557)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This form of time-travel is actually quite common, appearing in a fair number of popular stuff (Harry Potter, the British show 'Misfits', and Interstellar come to mind). I just don't find it all that interesting, and as others have said, the basic concept is all this has going for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to agree with mlister and Slambo. A retrocausal weapon that will be constructed in the future is very cool concept, but keeping it as a short article leaves only that concept and not a whole lot else when there could be room for some very cool stuff.

Novoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really cool, a clever use of time travel and an interesting way to apply the Thaumiel class.

I'm not sure if you wanted the non-link to blend in, but "main page" is too bright to look like an actual link. The color code should be bb0011. (You can see it on 3231, that's how I know. :V)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the concept, but imo the short length inhibits further expansion. Novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a very bare-bones article. It seems to ride on the concept, which just isn't enough for me, in this case.

item.title: The Once and Future Gun
item.content:encoded:
Move over, y'all. There's a new short Thaumiel in town.

Thanks to my reviewers: Draycott, slambo, weryllium, Lazar, Croquembouche, Gummy_Dragon, Arcas, DrChandra, MrBazzle, CaptainKirby

The image is from Document 1780-WL. FFB did not source it, but it is most likely their own creation.

If you liked this thing and would like to read some comments about it, or check out other things I've written, head to my author page for more!
